recipe.title: Friday: Classic Gazpacho with Grilled Ham & Cheese Sandwiches
ingredients: ripe tomatoes (about 6 medium — Roma are classic)
English cucumber, peeled and roughly chopped (reserve 1/4 cup finely diced for garnish)
red bell pepper, roughly chopped (seeds removed)
small Fresno chile or jalapeno, seeds removed (optional)
good sandwich bread (sourdough, Texas toast, or country white)
deli ham (honey ham or black forest)
Swiss, Gruyere, or sharp cheddar cheese
softened butter (or mayo for better browning)
steps: Add tomatoes, cucumber, bell pepper, onion, garlic, chile, olive oil, vinegar, honey, salt, pepper, and cilantro to a blender. Puree on high 60-90 seconds until smooth.
Transfer to sealed container. Refrigerate at least 2 hours.
Ladle into bowls; top with reserved diced cucumber, diced tomato, drizzle of olive oil, herb leaves.
Butter one side of each bread slice.
Build sandwiches: bread (butter side down), cheese, ham, cheese, bread (butter side up).
Heat skillet over medium-low. Cook 3-4 minutes per side until golden and cheese melts. Cover pan for second side.
Slice diagonally and serve alongside gazpacho.
